name: Dan Morgenstern
abstract: Dan Michael Morgenstern (October 24, 1929 – September 7, 2024) was an American jazz historian and archivist. Born to a Jewish family in Germany, Morgenstern fled Nazi-occupied Austria with his mother and in 1947 emigrated to the United States.

interviews.title: Dan Morgenstern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 9, 2013
Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

works_created.t: The Musical Life of Charlie Parker

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: And I met Dan Morgenstern at that time because Dan was on the board of the Jazz Museum. And we joke about it now because we’ve been friends for, you know, almost 50 years
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Stanley Dance
about_quotes.evidence: my friend Dan Morgenstern and his friend David Himmelstein, they got to the Little Theater in New York... And we were talking afterwards and they said, about whom they should have next, and I said well why don’t you get Earl Hines
about_quotes.doc_title: Stanley Dance interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Kirchner
about_quotes.evidence: I mean written by people like, well also I started reading DownBeat. You know I was reading all this stuff by people like Dan Morgenstern and Ira Gitler and Martin Williams, all of whom later became friends and colleagues of mine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Kirchner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] There’s a reference from Dan Morgenstern about the Swingsters. Was that ever a name for the group? ... [INTERVIEWER] Let me quote you on the liner notes. This is what Dan Morgenstern said – penned these.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Vincent Pelote
about_quotes.evidence: One of the first things I told Dan Morgenstern when I knew he was the Institute of Jazz Studies Director was, you know how many records I’ve bought strictly on the fact that you said it was a great record?
about_quotes.doc_title: Vincent Pelote interviewed by Monk Rowe, Orlando, Florida, February 25, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Leslie Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: when I saw Dan Morgenstern this last summer he came up to me and said, “Thank you for running the story on Thelma Terry I always wanted to know about her.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Leslie Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007
